title: Housing Search Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Housing Search Assistance 22 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Issues Domestic Violence Issues Former Foster Children Foster Children Hispanic/Latino Community Homeless Families Homeless Youth Homelessness Issues Teen Pregnancy Issues Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness BRADLEY ANGLE HOUSING ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides housing assistance. Assistance is based on funding availability and capacity. Assistance may involve helping survivors to: -Leave a dangerous place -Secure emergency safe nights and shelter access -Retain safe housing with eviction prevention -Transition to long-term or permanent housing -Emotional support, safety planning, and systems navigation support -Help with 14-day lease break letters -Address Confidentiality Program applications -Discussing housing options Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence within the last 180 calendar days. May offer additional services for Black, African-American, and LGBTQ2+ survivors of domestic violence when available. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm Email (503) 232-1528 303 Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT CAP HOUSING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides case management to help individuals find housing and related resources. Services include short- and long-term housing search support and one-time emergency rental assistance for security deposits or eviction prevention. 520 NW Davis Street Suite 215 Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Email (503) 223-5907 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD OASIS DROP-IN DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A collaborative project that provides homeless families priority access to a coordinated set of services offered at multiple locations in the G Street neighborhood. The participating organizations function as a campus, with all services within easy walking distance. Services include: -A safe place to be during the day -Access to laundry, showers, and restroom facilities -A place to prepare and eat simple meals -Help finding affordable housing and employment -Access to additional school and community based resources 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Households experiencing homelessness and at least one child age 17 and younger in their care Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-4pm, Wednesday 8am-6pm, closed holidays Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH COVO LOW-INCOME HOUSING FOR VETERANS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Housing units (a mix of single family homes and multi-family housing located in Bend, Oregon) are prioritized for homeless, low-income veterans. COVO also assists homeless, low-income veterans with housing search for rentals throughout Central, Oregon. 61510 S Highway 97 Suite 100 Bend, OR 977023706 Eligibility: Homeless Veterans who income-qualify and pass property management screening for tenants Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 383-2793 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION RESOURCE ENTERPRISES INC HOMELESS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides case management, transitional housing, rapid re-housing, permanent supportive housing, and veteran's housing services. Offers personal grooming items and shower vouchers when available. 2310 1st Street Suite 2 Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness or at risk of Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 842-5261 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY RECOVERY AND WELLNESS LIVING FULL WRAPAROUND SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides case management services to help connect people with services and resources that help with housing, homelessness, food, meal assistance, clothing and essentials, medical care, mental health services, drug and alcohol counseling, and peer support. 853 Medical Center Drive NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Friday 10am-noon and 1:30pm-5pm Email (971) 443-4176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMPASS HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support services that include advocacy, assistance navigating public services, meals, and help obtaining employment. Offers education assistance through tutoring and navigating educational resources in the community. The Clubhouse Model of rehabilitation is utilized. Membership is voluntary and without time limits. 37 N Ivy Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 18 and older with a diagnosed mental illness Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Tours: Monday-Friday 8am-3:30pm Meals: Varies call for information Email (541) 973-2840 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CROSSROADS COMMUNITIES APPLEGATE LANDING HOUSING ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides listings of affordable housing providers and can offer connections to possible housing opportunities. 1875 Stoltz Hill Road Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Households who are at low-to-moderate income standards, survivors of domestic violence, and individuals with severe and persistent mental illnesses. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm, Saturday 9am-5pm Email (541) 405-4210 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO HOME FINDER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides case management services and assistance in finding rental housing. 333 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 100 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 669-8350 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FORT KENNEDY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, pet food, clothing, showers, blood pressure checks, bus passes, legal information services, and benefit and housing resources. 7600 SE Johnson Creek Boulevard Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Veterans, all discharge types Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm. Various programs may have specific times. Email (503) 765-2661 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS WITH A MISSION MEDFORD YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Youth shelter providing temporary emergency shelter for up to 120 days with written parent/guardian consent or 72 hours without written consent. Services for sheltered and unsheltered youth include case management, mentoring, housing search assistance, and counseling services. Bathing facilities, laundry facilities, a clothing closet, and personal grooming supplies are also provided. School supplies are for enrolled participants only. 517 Edwards Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Homeless or at-risk youth ages 10-17 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 646-7385 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INSIGHTS YOUNG PARENT SERVICES HOMESAFE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Intensive case management. Case managers assist in obtaining services such as parenting classes, housing assistance, life skills training, health care, childcare, employment, or staying in school. 4900 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Houseless, single, pregnant or parenting birthing parent age 19 and younger Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 239-6996 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES NEW ROADS NEW ROADS DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in center with laundry facilities, phone use, temporary mailing address, showers, and daily meals. Also provides street outreach, case management, mental health counseling, education, and assistance finding housing. Eligible youth will be added to a central housing referral list for all programs they qualify for. 941 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Homeless and at-risk youth ages 16-24 Hours: -Drop-in Center: 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm -Breakfast: 7 days per week 8:30am-9:30am -Lunch: 7 days per week noon-1pm -Snack: 7 days per week 3pm-3:30pm -Dinner: 7 days per week 4pm-5pm Email (541) 686-4310 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID ROGUE FOUNDATION MOBILE NAVIGATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outreach assistance with housing and job search help, identification assistance (ID and Social Security cards), replacement birth certificate help with application and fee, general mail delivery, credit report requests, and SNAP and OHP application assistance. Also helps to connect people to services for savings, spending, educational plans, and life skills. Mobile Navigators meet clients in public spaces that work best for the client. PO Box 1972 Grants Pass, OR 97528 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 295-5706 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH NEW AVENUES COLUMBIA STREET TRANSITIONAL HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing and help with finding apartments, getting vouchers and subsidies, and connecting to other services. Housing options include The Moxie, a 24-bed facility in downtown Portland; Unity House, for LGBTQ2+; New Doors, for youth transitioning from foster care; New Meadows, a 15-unit community for youth formerly in foster care; and Butterfly House, for youth involved in the sex trade. 1220 SW Columbia Street Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Varies by program; age 25 and under experiencing homelessness or housing instability, with a focus on former foster youth, individuals with disabilities, and LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Not applicable Email (503) 517-3912 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CHILD WELFARE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Independent Living Program (ILP) helps youth who are, or were, in foster care to become self-sufficient adults. It gives an opportunity to learn valuable skills necessary to make a successful transition from state and Tribal custody to living on their own in the community. Services include access to case management and funds for continuing education and assistance with housing. 500 Summer Street NE Suite E67 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Current or former foster children ages 16-20 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 945-6969 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RESTORED CONNECTIONS PEER CENTER HOUSING SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance in transitioning out of homelessness, transitioning out of sheltered settings, support for recovery based housing options, and information and referral to assist in overcoming barriers to permanent housing. 146 E 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Individuals who are in recovery Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (458) 544-0000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEQUOIA MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE NETWORK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides low barrier treatment services for drug and alcohol addiction, assessment, treatment planning, individual/group counseling, case management, peer support, mentoring, specimen testing, supported employment, and housing resources. 4585 SW 185th Avenue Aloha, OR 97078 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday - Thursday 9am to 7pm and Friday 9am to 5pm Email (503) 591-9280 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEQUOIA MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES SEQUOIA MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES HILLSBORO BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE NETWORK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides low barrier treatment services for drug and alcohol addiction, assessment, treatment planning, individual/group counseling, case management, peer support, mentoring, specimen testing, supported employment, and housing resources. 395 W Main Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday - Thursday 9am to 7pm and Friday 9am to 5pm Email (503) 591-9280 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE WHITE RABBIT PROJECT WHITE RABBIT PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides housing stabilization services including tenancy support and case management. Services include crisis intervention, documentation support, landlord coordination, and connection to community services. Also offers compliance and emergency preparedness support for adult care home operators. Eligibility: Must be referred from North Clackamas School District’s McKinney-Vento program Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 927-3566 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to veterans in accessing VA and community services. Services may include short-term case management, onsite primary care, access to medical, mental health, and substance abuse treatment, and access to VA housing programs, including Grant and Per Diem program, HUD/VASH, Veterans Recovery House and Supportive Service for Veteran Families (SSVF) if found eligible. These grants do not provide immediate assistance, are sometimes one-time, and may include case management. Help with finding market rate or subsidized housing or linkage to community housing programs. Assistance with expediting VA Benefit claims and access to employment programs such as Supported Employment, Veterans Reintegration program, and State employment. Access to computers, clothing, and hygiene items is available onsite. 308 SW 1st Avenue Suite 155 Portland, OR 972043432 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless. Veterans must be present in the household and must be the one to request services. Some services available for Veterans who are not eligible for full VA Healthcare benefits. Hours: -Business hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 7:30am-4:30pm, Thursday 10am-4:30pm, closed federal holidays -Walk-in hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-3pm, Thursday 10am-3pm. No walk-ins after 2:30pm, better to go earlier in the day. Closed federal holidays. (503) 808-1256 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VETERANS STAND DOWNS AND VETERANS EVENTS STAND DOWN EVENTS VETERANS STAND DOWN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an annual health and wellness resource event. Services may include health screening, medical and dental care, vision, housing resources, counseling and substance abuse referrals, haircuts, pet care, job fairs, help accessing VA services and benefits, DMV and legal assistance. Meals, clothing, and hygiene supplies may also be offered. Eligibility: Veterans and military personnel; focus is generally on homeless and at-risk veterans Hours: Varies by event Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT CAP HOUSING SERVICES CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY OASIS DROP-IN DAY CENTER CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH COVO LOW-INCOME HOUSING FOR VETERANS COMMUNITY ACTION RESOURCE ENTERPRISES INC HOMELESS SERVICES COMMUNITY RECOVERY AND WELLNESS LIVING FULL WRAPAROUND SERVICES COMPASS HOUSE COMPASS HOUSE CROSSROADS COMMUNITIES HOUSING ADVOCACY EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO HOME FINDER SERVICES FORT KENNEDY FORT KENNEDY HEARTS WITH A MISSION YOUTH SHELTER INSIGHTS YOUNG PARENT SERVICES HOMESAFE PROGRAM LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES NEW ROADS DROP-IN CENTER MID ROGUE FOUNDATION MOBILE NAVIGATION PROGRAM NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH TRANSITIONAL HOUSING OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM RESTORED CONNECTIONS PEER CENTER HOUSING SUPPORT SEQUOIA MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE NETWORK SEQUOIA MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE NETWORK VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER
